notes: Items at the Roberts house. The chapter renders them with specificity — the World's Okayest Weaver mug, the bowl of pinecones from four Octobers ago, the foil-dimpled casserole, the wordless tapestry, the Polaroid. Mackenzie's win is not assembling an artifact; it is holding the morning. These items are present-and-handled, not bindle-equivalent.

worlds_okayest_mug.title: Mug · World's Okayest Weaver
worlds_okayest_mug.flavor: From your sister, three Christmases ago. She meant it ironically. You use it sincerely. The chip on the rim is from a morning you have already forgotten.

philips_dishtowel.title: Dishtowel · clean an hour ago
philips_dishtowel.flavor: Philip uses it for everything. He has used it for everything since 2019.

the_wrench.title: The Wrench
the_wrench.flavor: On the counter. Philip set it down with the small careful gesture of a man who has just lost an argument he was not, technically, having out loud.

the_polaroid.title: Polaroid · June 2009 · Houston Street
the_polaroid.flavor: A faded Polaroid. Houston Street in late-afternoon light. A young man walking with his back to the camera, hands in his pockets. On the back, in handwriting nobody in the house recognizes: He waved.

the_pinecones.title: Bowl of Pinecones · four Octobers ago
the_pinecones.flavor: From a hike four Octobers ago. Mackenzie never threw them out. Philip has thought about throwing them out and has not.

the_unopened_mail.title: Unopened Mail
the_unopened_mail.flavor: A credit-card statement. A flyer for the storm-drain crew. A wedding invitation from somebody you'll get to. You'll get to it.

the_keys.title: The Keys
the_keys.flavor: House key on the brass ring. Car key. The smaller key whose lock neither of you can remember. You have not, in three years, thrown that smaller key out.

the_returned_casserole.title: Casserole · returned uneaten
the_returned_casserole.flavor: Foil-wrapped, dimpled in the precise pattern of the fingers that pressed it back over the dish without unwrapping it. In the freezer since Friday. You and Philip have not discussed it.

the_work_in_progress.title: Work in Progress · 3 inches
the_work_in_progress.flavor: Three inches into something you have not, yet, named. The threads picked back up after the fourteen-month gap. The piece will be what it will be. You will know what it is when you reach six inches.

the_yarn_basket.title: Yarn Basket
the_yarn_basket.flavor: The colors Mackenzie has chosen for the next month. Indigo, oak-bark brown, the off-white she dyes with onion skins from her sister's husband's onions.

the_driftwood_sculpture_in_progress.title: Driftwood Sculpture · in progress
the_driftwood_sculpture_in_progress.flavor: Philip's. Under the eaves. Three pieces of salvaged driftwood that, when held in the right relation, look like a person. Philip has not, yet, let you see how he holds them.

the_rosemary_bush.title: Rosemary · from Philip's mother
the_rosemary_bush.flavor: The bush Philip's mother gave you the year before she went into the home. It came in a small terracotta pot. It is now five feet across.
